# Test bash coverage collection via BASH_XTRACEFD
env MCP_BASH_COVERAGE=1
env MCP_BASH_COVERAGE_DIR=$WORK/bash-coverage

# Make test scripts executable
exec chmod 0755 test_script.sh
exec chmod 0755 complex_script.sh
exec chmod 0755 tool_script.sh

# Run bash commands with coverage enabled
bash './test_script.sh hello'
stdout 'Starting test'
stdout 'Hello, world!'
stdout 'Test complete'

# Check that trace was recorded
exec ls $WORK/bash-coverage/
stdout 'bash-unknown'
stdout '.trace'

# Run another command
bash './test_script.sh goodbye'
stdout 'Starting test'
stdout 'Goodbye, world!'
stdout 'Test complete'

# Verify we have multiple trace files
exec bash -c 'ls $WORK/bash-coverage/ | wc -l'
stdout '2'

# Run the complex script
bash './complex_script.sh'
stdout 'Hello, Alice!'
stdout 'Hello, Bob!'
stdout 'Hello, Charlie!'

# Test with a script that calls other tools
bash './tool_script.sh'
stdout 'Testing tool integration'
stdout 'Tool test complete'

# Disable coverage and run a command
env MCP_BASH_COVERAGE=0
bash 'echo "No coverage here"'
stdout 'No coverage here'

# Verify no new trace files were created (should be 4 from previous runs)
exec bash -c 'ls $WORK/bash-coverage/ | wc -l'
stdout '4'

-- test_script.sh --
#!/bin/bash
echo "Starting test"
if [ "$1" = "hello" ]; then
    echo "Hello, world!"
else
    echo "Goodbye, world!"
fi
echo "Test complete"

-- complex_script.sh --
#!/bin/bash
function greet() {
    echo "Hello, $1!"
}

for name in Alice Bob Charlie; do
    greet $name
done

if command -v mcpdiff &> /dev/null; then
    echo "mcpdiff is available"
fi

-- tool_script.sh --
#!/bin/bash
echo "Testing tool integration"
# This would call mcpdiff if it was built
# mcpdiff file1.mcp file2.mcp
echo "Tool test complete"
